_instructions: This file controls all game content. Edit it using Claude Desktop by asking Claude to add, remove, or change scenarios. Each scenario has a type: 'choice', 'spot', or 'reaction'. See CONTENT_GUIDE.md for full instructions.

coach.name: Coach Zara
corrections: Good try! Think about who has more space. 💭
Almost! Look for the open player next time. 👀
Nice effort! The goal was open on that one. 🥅
Keep going! You'll spot it quicker next time. 💪
So close! Think about where the defender is. 🤔

scenarios.question: You have the ball near midfield. A defender is running at you fast! What do you do?

choices.text: Pass to Emma on the left
choices.explanation: Emma is wide open with no defenders near her. Great vision!

choices.text: Try to dribble past the defender
choices.explanation: The defender is too close — you'd likely lose the ball!

choices.text: Pass to Sofia on the right
choices.explanation: Sofia has a defender right next to her. Emma was the better option.

choices.text: Kick it out of bounds
choices.explanation: We never give up possession on purpose when there's a free teammate!

scenarios.question: You're inside the penalty box with the ball. The goalkeeper moved to the left side. Where do you shoot?

choices.text: Shoot to the right side of the goal
choices.explanation: The keeper moved left, so the right side is wide open. Smart shooting!

choices.text: Shoot to the left where the keeper is
choices.explanation: Always shoot away from the goalkeeper — aim for the open side!

choices.text: Pass back to a midfielder
choices.explanation: You're in a great shooting position! Take the shot when you have a clear chance.

choices.text: Dribble closer first
choices.explanation: You're already in range — taking more touches gives defenders time to recover!

scenarios.question: The other team has the ball near your goal. Your teammate is between you and the attacker. What should you do?

choices.text: Stay in position and cover the space behind Jess
choices.explanation: Great defensive thinking! If the attacker gets past Jess, you're the last line of defense.

choices.text: Run forward to help Jess tackle
choices.explanation: If both you and Jess go for the ball, the attacker can pass and beat you both!

choices.text: Run towards your own goal
choices.explanation: Stay goal-side but don't retreat too far — hold your position and be ready.

choices.text: Call for the ball to start an attack
choices.explanation: We're defending right now — focus on stopping their attack first!

scenarios.question: Tap the teammate who is most open to receive a pass!
scenarios.explanation: Anna on the left had no defenders near her — she was the open option!

scenarios.question: TAP when you see a GREEN light — don't tap RED!
You're being pressed by two defenders. You have two seconds to decide — where does the ball go?

choices.text: Pass to Ria — she's closest
choices.explanation: Closest doesn't always mean best! A defender is right next to Ria.

choices.text: Pass forward to Tess
choices.explanation: Excellent! Tess is in space and moving forward — great vision under pressure!

choices.text: Pass back to Kim
choices.explanation: Kim is open too, but playing backwards when Tess is free going forward loses momentum.

choices.text: Hold the ball and wait
choices.explanation: Two defenders pressing means you must release the ball quickly — trust your teammates!

scenarios.question: Quick! Tap the MOST DANGEROUS attacker your team needs to mark!
scenarios.explanation: ATK2 is closest to goal with a clear path — she needed marking first!

scenarios.question: TAP the SOCCER BALL — ignore the other shapes!
You're one-on-one with the goalkeeper! She's coming out fast. What's the best move?

choices.text: Shoot early before she reaches you
choices.explanation: When the keeper comes out, shoot early while you still have angle — brilliant composure!

choices.text: Try to dribble around the keeper
choices.explanation: She's coming fast — dribbling takes too long. A quick shot is better here.

choices.text: Pass wide to Faye
choices.explanation: Faye is an option, but you have a clear shooting chance — take it with confidence!

choices.text: Stop and wait for her to commit
choices.explanation: If you wait too long, she'll close the angle completely. Be decisive!

scenarios.question: Your team has a corner kick. You're in the box. Where's the best place to stand?

choices.text: Stand at the far post, away from defenders
choices.explanation: The far post is often left unmarked. Great movement to find space!

choices.text: Stand in the middle of the goal mouth with everyone else
choices.explanation: That's where defenders are watching. Find space away from them!

choices.text: Stand outside the box and wait
choices.explanation: You want to be ready to receive and score — get into the box!

choices.text: Stand right next to the goalkeeper
choices.explanation: The keeper controls that area. Move away from her to find space!

scenarios.question: You receive the ball with your back to the opponent's goal. A defender is right behind you. What do you do?

choices.text: Lay it off to Jade who is beside you
choices.explanation: Smart! Laying the ball off quickly to a nearby teammate keeps possession and relieves pressure.

choices.text: Try to turn and take on the defender
choices.explanation: Turning into a tight defender is risky — you could lose the ball easily!

choices.text: Hold the ball and wait for someone to call
choices.explanation: With a defender on your back, holding too long means you'll get tackled. Move it quickly!

choices.text: Kick it long forward without looking
choices.explanation: Kicking blind can give the ball straight to their defenders. Check your options first!

scenarios.question: An attacker is running at you with the ball. What is the best way to defend?

choices.text: Stay on your feet, slow her down, and wait for help
choices.explanation: Staying on your feet and being patient is the best defending — don't dive in!

choices.text: Dive in and try to kick the ball away
choices.explanation: Diving in is risky! If you miss, the attacker is free to run at goal.

choices.text: Back away as fast as possible
choices.explanation: Backing off too much gives the attacker space to shoot. Stay close but controlled.

choices.text: Run past her to get to the ball
choices.explanation: Running past the attacker leaves the goal wide open behind you!

scenarios.question: Your team is attacking and you don't have the ball. Where should you move to be most helpful?

choices.text: Find a gap between the defenders and call for the ball
choices.explanation: Creating space between defenders gives your teammate a great passing option. Brilliant movement!

choices.text: Stand still and watch the play
choices.explanation: Always keep moving when your team has the ball — make yourself an option!

choices.text: Run back towards your own half
choices.explanation: Your team is attacking — stay forward and look for space to receive!

choices.text: Run to where Priya already is
choices.explanation: Bunching together makes it easy for defenders. Spread out to create more options!

scenarios.question: Your team is defending. Tap the teammate who is in the WORST position right now!
scenarios.explanation: Liv is way out on the right with no attacker to mark — she needs to come in and help defend the middle!

scenarios.question: You're just outside the penalty box with the ball and no defender is close. What do you do?

choices.text: Take a shot at goal — you have space!
choices.explanation: You have time, space, and you're in range — take the shot with confidence!

choices.text: Wait for a defender to come before deciding
choices.explanation: Waiting gives defenders time to close you down. Shoot while you have space!

choices.text: Pass sideways to Cleo
choices.explanation: Cleo is in a similar position and no closer to goal — you have the best shot right now!

choices.text: Dribble backwards away from goal
choices.explanation: Never go backwards when you have a clear shooting chance — be brave and shoot!

scenarios.question: The other team is about to take a free kick near the edge of your box. Where do you stand?

choices.text: Get into the defensive wall to block the shot
choices.explanation: Standing in the wall helps block direct shots at goal. Great defensive awareness!

choices.text: Run forward to press the player taking the kick
choices.explanation: You can't press during a free kick — you must stay back until the ball is played.

choices.text: Stand near the halfway line in case of a counter-attack
choices.explanation: Your team needs all defenders near the box — stay close to protect the goal!

choices.text: Stand behind the goalkeeper
choices.explanation: Behind the keeper is out of play — you need to be in the wall or marking an attacker.

scenarios.question: Your team just won the ball. Tap the teammate who is in the best position to start the attack!
scenarios.explanation: Dani is ahead of the ball, wide open on the left, with space to run at goal — the perfect outlet!

scenarios.question: Your team loses the ball in the opponent's half. What should you do immediately?

choices.text: Sprint back towards your own half immediately
choices.explanation: Transitioning quickly when you lose the ball stops counter-attacks before they start. Great discipline!

choices.text: Stay forward and wait for your team to win it back
choices.explanation: Staying up when your team loses the ball leaves space for the opponent to attack through.

choices.text: Argue with your teammate about the mistake
choices.explanation: Focus on the game! Sprint back first, talk about it at half-time.

choices.text: Walk back slowly to save energy
choices.explanation: Counter-attacks happen in seconds — you need to sprint back, not walk!

scenarios.question: The goalkeeper on your team has the ball and is looking to start an attack. Where should you move to help?

choices.text: Run wide to give the keeper a clear passing lane
choices.explanation: Moving wide creates a simple short pass for the keeper and gets the attack going quickly!

choices.text: Stay in the middle where you are
choices.explanation: You're in a crowded central area. Moving wide gives the keeper more options!

choices.text: Run towards the keeper to get the ball
choices.explanation: Running towards the keeper brings your defender with you and makes the pass harder!

choices.text: Sprint into the opponent's half straight away
choices.explanation: You're too far away for a long kick to reach you safely. Stay closer and build up the play!

scenarios.question: Tap the teammate who has the most space to receive a through ball!
scenarios.explanation: Lena is running into space up the middle with no defender close — a perfectly timed through ball would send her clear!

scenarios.question: Two attackers are running towards your goal and there's only you to stop them. What do you do?

choices.text: Stay central, delay both attackers, and don't commit
choices.explanation: By staying central you cover both options and force the attacker to make a risky decision. Smart defending!

choices.text: Sprint at the player with the ball and tackle hard
choices.explanation: If you dive in and miss, the other attacker is free to score. Stay on your feet!

choices.text: Mark the attacker without the ball
choices.explanation: The player with the ball is the danger — keep your eye on her first!

choices.text: Retreat all the way to the goal line
choices.explanation: Retreating too much lets them get very close before shooting. Stay between them and slow them down!

scenarios.question: You receive a cross in the penalty box. The ball is coming to your weaker foot. What do you do?

choices.text: Shoot first time with your weaker foot — you're in a great spot!
choices.explanation: Brave! In the box, shooting quickly with any foot is better than hesitating and losing the chance.

choices.text: Wait and try to switch the ball to your stronger foot
choices.explanation: Taking extra touches in the box gives defenders time to block you. Shoot quickly!

choices.text: Pass back out to Kit
choices.explanation: You're in a prime scoring position — believe in yourself and take the shot!

choices.text: Try to control the ball and dribble towards the keeper
choices.explanation: Dribbling in a crowded box is risky. First-time shots catch goalkeepers off-guard!

scenarios.question: Your team has a throw-in in your own half. The opponents are pressing high. What's the safest option?

choices.text: Short throw to Gabi who has dropped free behind you
choices.explanation: Gabi has space and you can build from the back safely — great awareness!

choices.text: Long throw forward into a crowded midfield
choices.explanation: Throwing long into pressure risks losing the ball. Keep it simple and safe!

choices.text: Throw it straight to a defender being pressed
choices.explanation: Throwing into pressure is risky. Find the free player first!

choices.text: Wait and hope someone calls for it
choices.explanation: Look quickly and find the free player yourself — don't stand still with the ball!

scenarios.question: Your team is about to lose the ball. Tap the defender who is out of position!
scenarios.explanation: Vee is stranded on the far right with three attackers threatening central — she needs to get back into position fast!

scenarios.question: You're dribbling down the wing and the defender is blocking your path to cross. What's the smartest move?

choices.text: Cut back and pass to Nell in the middle
choices.explanation: The defender blocked the wing — cutting back to Nell keeps possession and keeps the attack alive. Clever!

choices.text: Try to beat the defender and cross anyway
choices.explanation: The defender is well-placed — the cut-back to Nell is the smarter option here.

choices.text: Stop and hold the ball until someone makes a run
choices.explanation: Stopping in the corner lets defenders surround you. Keep the ball moving!

choices.text: Pass backwards all the way to your goalkeeper
choices.explanation: Going all the way back loses attacking momentum when Nell is available in a great spot!

scenarios.question: The ball is on the opposite side of the field from you. How should you position yourself?

choices.text: Move slightly towards the centre, ready to switch play
choices.explanation: Tucking in slightly when the ball is far away means you're ready if the ball is switched to your side. Great shape!

choices.text: Stay as wide as possible on the right
choices.explanation: Being too wide when the ball is on the opposite side means you're too far from the action if it switches.

choices.text: Run to the left side where the ball is
choices.explanation: If you all crowd one side, there's no one available when the ball switches. Hold your position!

choices.text: Drop back to help defend
choices.explanation: Your team is attacking — stay forward so there's an option when they switch the play to your side!

scenarios.question: The goalkeeper kicked the ball long. Tap the teammate who is best placed to win the header!
scenarios.explanation: Drew is right where the ball is dropping and has a step on the nearest defender — she's best placed to win the header!

scenarios.question: The other team's striker is running towards you with the ball but hasn't looked up yet. What do you do?

choices.text: Get close and show her towards the outside
choices.explanation: Showing an attacker towards the outside moves the danger away from the centre of goal. Textbook defending!

choices.text: Back off and give her space to shoot
choices.explanation: Giving too much space lets the attacker get a clean shot off. Stay close and make it hard!

choices.text: Slide tackle immediately
choices.explanation: A slide tackle when she hasn't committed is too risky — you might miss and foul her!

choices.text: Shout at your goalkeeper to come out
choices.explanation: The keeper should stay on her line — it's your job to deal with this. Stay focused!

scenarios.question: Your team wins a penalty kick! You're about to take it. The keeper is standing in the centre. What's your plan?

choices.text: Pick a corner and shoot with power and accuracy
choices.explanation: Picking a corner and committing to it is the best penalty strategy — keepers can't cover the corners if you're accurate!

choices.text: Wait to see which way the keeper dives, then shoot the other way
choices.explanation: Waiting for the keeper to move slows you down and makes the kick less powerful. Decide before you run up!

choices.text: Aim straight down the middle
choices.explanation: Shooting down the middle can work but is easily saved if the keeper stands still. Aim for a corner!

choices.text: Try a soft chip down the middle
choices.explanation: A chip is very risky — if the keeper doesn't dive, she'll easily save it. Shoot with confidence!

scenarios.question: You have the ball at your feet and your teammate is calling for it — but an opponent is between you both. What do you do?

choices.text: Pass to Nadia who is open on the left instead
choices.explanation: Even though Lexi called, the defender is blocking that pass. Finding the open player is smarter!

choices.text: Force the pass through to Lexi anyway
choices.explanation: A defender is right in the way — the pass will be intercepted! Find the free player.

choices.text: Dribble towards the defender to create space
choices.explanation: Nadia is free right now — pass to her before the chance disappears!

choices.text: Shout at Lexi to move
choices.explanation: There's no time to shout instructions — play the easy ball to Nadia and keep moving!

scenarios.question: Your team just scored! Tap the teammate you should hug first — she made the key assist run!
scenarios.explanation: Rue made a brilliant run to the far post to pull the defender away and create the space for the goal — she made it happen!

scenarios.question: You're playing as a striker and your team is defending a corner. Where should you position yourself?

choices.text: Stay near the halfway line ready for a quick counter-attack
choices.explanation: As a striker, your job on corners is to be the counter-attack threat. Stay forward and be ready to run!

choices.text: Go back into your own box to help defend
choices.explanation: Your team has defenders for that! Your job as striker is to be ready for the counter.

choices.text: Stand right next to the corner flag to block the kick
choices.explanation: You can't block a corner kick — that's not a rule you can use here!

choices.text: Go to the edge of the penalty box and wait
choices.explanation: If the ball is cleared, it probably won't reach that far. Get into a real counter-attack position!

scenarios.question: You're through on goal but you're running at full speed. The goalkeeper is rushing out. What do you do?

choices.text: Stay calm and chip the ball over the rushing keeper
choices.explanation: A chip over a rushing keeper is a brilliant finish — it uses her own momentum against her!

choices.text: Try to run around the goalkeeper
choices.explanation: At full speed it's very hard to go around a keeper who's already rushing out. The chip is smarter!

choices.text: Blast the ball as hard as possible
choices.explanation: Blasting into a keeper who's rushing towards you often leads to a block. Stay calm and chip!

choices.text: Pass sideways to Juno at the last second
choices.explanation: Juno is a backup option, but you have the best chance right now — back yourself and go for goal!

scenarios.question: Tap the opponent who is completely unmarked and most dangerous right now!
scenarios.explanation: ATK3 has no one near her on the right side and is in a great position to receive the ball and shoot — she needs marking immediately!

scenarios.question: The other team's winger is running fast down the side with the ball. You're the last defender. What do you do?

choices.text: Stay between the attacker and the goal, slow her down
choices.explanation: As the last defender, your goal is to delay and protect the goal — not to win the ball at all costs!

choices.text: Sprint at the ball and try to tackle
choices.explanation: If you miss the tackle as last defender, she's free to score. Don't gamble — delay!

choices.text: Foul her to stop the attack
choices.explanation: Fouling could give away a penalty or free kick in a dangerous area. Stay on your feet!

choices.text: Let her go and shout for offside
choices.explanation: You can't just let her go — stay close and force her wide, away from goal!

scenarios.question: You're in midfield. You have the ball but three options: go forward, sideways, or back. The pressure is low. What do you choose?

choices.text: Pass forward to Rena who is ahead of you
choices.explanation: When you have space and a teammate forward, progress the ball! Rena is open and moving forward — brilliant decision!

choices.text: Pass sideways to Cori to recycle possession
choices.explanation: Sideways is fine when under pressure, but Rena is free going forward — always look to progress first!

choices.text: Pass back to Petra to keep it safe
choices.explanation: Playing backwards when you have a forward pass available slows the attack. Look forward first!

choices.text: Dribble forward yourself
choices.explanation: Dribbling when a teammate is free is usually slower — pass and move for better team play!

scenarios.question: Your team has a goal kick. Tap the teammate in the best position to receive it safely!
scenarios.explanation: Willa has dropped to the left side and has no one near her — she's the safest and clearest option for the goal kick!

scenarios.question: Your team is about to defend a goal kick from the other team. Where should you stand?

choices.text: Press up close to their player to try to win the ball in the air
choices.explanation: Getting tight to your opponent and challenging for the ball means you might win it and start an attack. Great pressing!

choices.text: Stay back near your own goal just in case
choices.explanation: Dropping too deep gives the other team too much space to build their attack freely.

choices.text: Stand at the halfway line and wait
choices.explanation: Waiting at the halfway line lets their team receive the ball and run at you. Press up and challenge!

choices.text: Run behind your own goalkeeper
choices.explanation: That's too far back! You need to be in a position to challenge for the ball or be ready to defend.
